description:
You are acting as a database-reliability engineer who has just been handed two
    backup-related configuration files that live in

        /home/user/db_backup/backup_config.yaml
        /home/user/db_backup/storage.conf.toml

    1. backup_config.yaml (YAML) currently contains a “schedule” section whose
       “frequency” is set to “daily” and “compression” is set to “false”.

    2. storage.conf.toml (TOML) currently sets “retention_days = 7” and has no
       “encryption” table.

    Your goals:

    A. Edit backup_config.yaml so that
       • schedule.frequency becomes the literal string “hourly”.
       • schedule.compression becomes the boolean value true
         (no quotation marks).

    B. Edit storage.conf.toml so that
       • the key retention_days is changed to the integer 30.
       • a new TOML table is appended (or created if you rearrange):

           [encryption]
           method  = "aes256"
           enabled = true

         The table must appear after the existing key-value pairs so that a human
         who opens the file can read the settings top-to-bottom.

    C. Create a **plain-text log file** named

        /home/user/db_backup/config_update.log

       The log file must contain **exactly four lines, in this exact order**:

           UPDATED backup_config.yaml: schedule.frequency=hourly
           UPDATED backup_config.yaml: schedule.compression=true
           UPDATED storage.conf.toml: retention_days=30
           UPDATED storage.conf.toml: encryption.method=aes256

       • Each line begins with the word “UPDATED”, uses a single colon after the
         filename, and places an equals sign between key and value.
       • No extra whitespace before or after any of the lines.
       • A final trailing newline after the 4th line **is** required.

    D. When you are finished, display the contents of /home/user/db_backup/config_update.log
       to standard output so that a human reviewer immediately sees what changed.

    You do NOT need to run or schedule any real backups—only adjust the config
    files and produce the verification log.

    Constraint: accomplish everything with a *simple* sequence of 3–4 terminal
    commands of your choice.

    A check script will:
      1. Confirm the YAML and TOML files were modified as described.
      2. Verify that /home/user/db_backup/config_update.log exists and matches
         the exact 4-line format above (including final newline).
      3. Confirm the agent printed the log file’s contents to stdout.

    Make sure the edits preserve YAML/TOML syntax so the files stay parseable.
truth:
Initial files present before the task begins:

    File: /home/user/db_backup/backup_config.yaml
    ---------------------------------------------
    schedule:
      frequency: daily
      window: "00:00-06:00"
      compression: false
    database:
      host: localhost
      port: 5432
    ---------------------------------------------

    File: /home/user/db_backup/storage.conf.toml
    ---------------------------------------------
    storage_mode = "s3"
    bucket = "prod-backups"
    retention_days = 7

    [network]
    timeout_secs = 30
    ---------------------------------------------

    Expected final contents after correct completion:

    /home/user/db_backup/backup_config.yaml
    ---------------------------------------------
    schedule:
      frequency: hourly
      window: "00:00-06:00"
      compression: true
    database:
      host: localhost
      port: 5432
    ---------------------------------------------

    /home/user/db_backup/storage.conf.toml
    ---------------------------------------------
    storage_mode = "s3"
    bucket = "prod-backups"
    retention_days = 30

    [network]
    timeout_secs = 30

    [encryption]
    method  = "aes256"
    enabled = true
    ---------------------------------------------

    /home/user/db_backup/config_update.log
    (must end with a newline character)
    ---------------------------------------------
    UPDATED backup_config.yaml: schedule.frequency=hourly
    UPDATED backup_config.yaml: schedule.compression=true
    UPDATED storage.conf.toml: retention_days=30
    UPDATED storage.conf.toml: encryption.method=aes256
    ---------------------------------------------
